evidence.description: Executed deterministic graders produce the recorded pass/fail outcomes on four builder-authored planted fixtures.
limitations: Calibration fixtures and failure signatures are internally authored.
Four fixtures do not define or sample a target population.
No expert adjudication, reliability estimate, or semantic-entailment study was performed.
evidence.reviewer: benchmark-builder synthetic execution; not independent review

evaluation_context.configured_system_population: The checked-in deterministic grader implementations only; no agent system is evaluated.
evaluation_context.task_domain_population: Exactly four builder-authored LH adoption calibration fixtures.
evaluation_context.environment_tools: Local Python execution against checked-in CSV and Markdown fixtures.
evaluation_context.time_window: Repository versions and hashes recorded on 2026-07-10.

claim.statement: The pinned deterministic graders emit the recorded expected outcomes on the four named planted fixtures.
claim.object_definition: Exact equality between rerun check outcomes and the outcome table in the immutable calibration measurement.
claim.intended_interpretation: A narrow executable regression claim about these implementations and fixtures.
claim.intended_use: Detect code changes that alter the planted-fixture outcome contract.
excluded_interpretations: The graders are accurate or reliable on a target population.
The graders measure professional judgment or agent capability.
The procedural skill improves performance or is deployment-ready.
claim.generalization_boundary: No generalization beyond the four named fixtures, pinned component hashes, and local deterministic execution.

inference.warrant: The claimed criterion is the directly rerunnable equality represented by the synthetic measurement; no latent construct inference is made.

assumptions.text: The referenced hashes identify the executed artifacts.

rebuttals.text: Co-authored fixtures could make pass/fail outcomes tautological.
rebuttals.response: Claim scope is restricted to regression behavior and excludes accuracy, validity, and population performance.

validity_facets.rationale: The claim enumerates exactly the four fixture cases represented in the measurement.
The measurement and claim object are the same exact outcome-equality criterion; this does not validate scoring reliability beyond these cases.
No latent professional capability, judgment, or grader-quality construct is claimed.
The external boundary is deliberately closed to the pinned local fixtures and versions rather than extrapolated.
The intended use is an internal regression alarm with no capability or release decision.

reassessment_triggers: Fixture set or expected outcomes change

evaluation_context.task_domain_population: Four synthetic calibration fixtures in one unreleased pilot.
evaluation_context.environment_tools: Task-only fixture replay; not a valid capability trial.

claim.statement: The evaluated agent systems can perform evidence-grounded professional adoption analysis.
claim.object_definition: A latent capability spanning evidence retrieval, judgment, artifact construction, and decision support in realistic work.
claim.intended_interpretation: General professional capability rather than fixture conformity.
claim.intended_use: Compare configured agents or procedural-skill treatments.
excluded_interpretations: Capability outside validated domains and environments
claim.generalization_boundary: No defensible population boundary is established by the current evidence.

inference.warrant: No warrant currently bridges synthetic grader behavior to professional capability.

assumptions.text: The four co-authored fixtures represent the professional capability construct.

rebuttals.text: The replay contains no valid matched live-agent contrast.
rebuttals.response: Keep the construct claim unsupported pending valid trials and convergent/discriminant evidence.

unresolved_evidence: Expert-reviewed construct map and domain coverage
validity_facets.rationale: Four co-authored fixtures cannot establish coverage.

unresolved_evidence: Comparison with authentic expert work outcomes
validity_facets.rationale: No external professional criterion was measured.

unresolved_evidence: Convergent, discriminant, process, and alternative-cause evidence
validity_facets.rationale: Deterministic convention checks do not isolate professional capability.

unresolved_evidence: Valid agent trials across tasks, systems, and environments
validity_facets.rationale: The synthetic measurement explicitly excludes agent capability and population generalization.
This argument makes no operational decision claim.

reassessment_triggers: Expert construct and artifact review complete

evaluation_context.configured_system_population: No deployment candidate population is represented.
evaluation_context.environment_tools: Fixture replay with no operational workflow or affected-user evidence.

claim.statement: A configured agent should be approved for professional deployment based on these grader results.
claim.object_definition: A go/no-go deployment action with acceptable downstream loss.
excluded_interpretations: A passing synthetic check is sufficient evidence of safety or usefulness
claim.generalization_boundary: No deployment setting, population, or risk class has been validated.

inference.warrant: No supported warrant links fixture outcomes to acceptable deployment loss.

assumptions.text: Synthetic fixture outcomes predict safe and useful deployment.

rebuttals.text: No empirical loss study or expert standard setting supports a decision threshold.
rebuttals.response: Keep readiness unsupported and prohibit release use.

validity_facets.rationale: Synthetic cases do not cover deployment demands.

unresolved_evidence: Population, institution, tool, and temporal transfer
Affected-stakeholder review, impact audit, error costs, and residual-risk acceptance
validity_facets.rationale: The measurement excludes readiness use and supplies no impact evidence.

decision_policy.action: Do not approve deployment from this evidence.
decision_policy.loss_basis: Unknown; false approval could expose users to unsupported professional recommendations.
decision_policy.residual_risk: Unbounded because capability, safety, and consequence evidence are absent.
mitigations: Collect valid trials and stakeholder-specific loss evidence before proposing a threshold

reassessment_triggers: A deployment context and affected stakeholders are named
Threshold and loss evidence is available
